name: security-advisory-context-shape
doc: check-skill.ts's securityAdvisory (PreToolUse, scope=security) used to hand-roll its OWN JSON with `permissionDecision: "allow"` ALONGSIDE `additionalContext` -- unlike every other non-blocking advisory in the codebase, which renders through the shared adapters/claude contextResponse builder (`additionalContext` only, no `permissionDecision`). Codex's own hook shape (adapters/codex/index.ts) never emits a `permissionDecision` for a non-blocking inform -- this scenario proves the fix: a fresh session's first Write to a code file under scope=security still gets the SECURITY advisory (non-blocking, exit 0), but the rendered JSON now carries `additionalContext` WITHOUT any `permissionDecision` field at all.

steps.doc: Fresh session (no security-state file yet under $TMP/.claude/logs/00-security/), Write on a .ts file -> the advisory fires: stdout contains the SECURITY nudge, is valid additionalContext JSON, and carries NO `permissionDecision` field (the exact regression this scenario guards).
Same event again: the rendered JSON must not contain `permissionDecision` anywhere (regex asserts the substring never occurs across the whole stdout).
A non-code file (.md) never triggers the advisory at all -- unaffected by the fix, still a silent allow.
